name: Sauce Poivrade
recipeIngredient: 1 large leek, white only, thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Sauté the bacon in a large heavy saucepan over medium heat for 2 minutes.
2. Add the onions, carrots, shallots, and leek; cook until well browned, about 12 to 15 minutes.
3. Stir in the vinegar and simmer over low heat until the liquid has evaporated, about 10 to 15 minutes.
4. Whisk in the flour and cook one minute more.
5. Add the garlic, wine, bouquet garni, and stock; simmer over low heat for one hour.
6. Stir in the cracked peppercorns and salt; then simmer for an additional 10 minutes.
7. Strain through a sieve lined with cheesecloth.
8. Return to the saucepan and add the brandy. Simmer three minutes over low heat before serving hot.
This easy version of the classic pepper sauce clearly illustrates why some old-time combinations just cannot be improved upon. Serve this with beef or game, especially pheasant. This sauce will also keep for two days in the refrigerator. Re-warm over low heat before serving.
